name: Amscot - The Money Superstore
description_short: Florida-based lender offering payday loans (cash advances) up to $500 and installment loans up to $1,000 with no credit checks and same-day funding at 365 physical locations.
description_long:
Amscot Financial, operating as "The Money Superstore," has been providing short-term lending services to Florida residents since 1989. The company operates exclusively in Florida, where state regulations include some of the strictest consumer-protection laws in the United States. With locations open 365 days a year (many 24 hours), Amscot has built a presence serving millions of Floridians seeking quick access to emergency cash.

Amscot offers two primary loan products: Cash Advances (payday loans) up to $500 and Installment Cash Advances up to $1,000. Both products advertise no credit checks, simple application processes, and same-day cash disbursement. Beyond lending, the company provides ancillary financial services including check cashing, free money orders, wire transfers, bill payment, faxing, copying, stamp sales, notarization, and ATM access. The company is licensed by the Florida Office of Financial Regulation (OFR), registered with FinCEN as a Money Service Business, and adheres to INFiN's "Best Practices" standards.

Amscot distinguishes itself through physical convenience (365-day operations, many 24-hour locations, walk-out-with-cash service) and accessibility for consumers with poor or no credit history. The company explicitly markets its lack of credit checks and fast approval process. As a member of INFiN, Amscot complies with federal restrictions prohibiting loans to active military members, their spouses, or dependents.

While Amscot provides genuine emergency cash access, consumers should recognize that payday loans and installment cash advances are high-cost borrowing products typically carrying significant APRs and fees not disclosed on this location page. The website notes that "some restrictions apply," "not all customers will qualify," and "some consumers may only be eligible for $50," indicating lending decisions are individualized and approval is not guaranteed despite marketing claims of simplicity.
pros: Open 365 days a year with many 24-hour locations for maximum accessibility
No credit checks required for either Cash Advances or Installment Cash Advances
Walk out with physical cash in hand in minutes, not a check or pending transfer
Installment loans up to $1,000 offer more borrowing capacity and longer repayment than standard payday loans
Licensed by Florida OFR and regulated by state and federal payday loan laws
Member of INFiN and adheres to industry best practices standards
Wide range of ancillary services (check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, notarization, ATM access)
cons: Payday and installment loans are inherently high-cost products with significant APRs and fees (not disclosed on this page)
Approval not guaranteed despite marketing language; some applicants may qualify for only $50 maximum
Available only in Florida, limiting geographic access for consumers in other states
Short repayment terms on cash advances create risk of repeat borrowing and debt cycles
Federal military lending restrictions (Servicemembers Civil Relief Act compliance) exclude active military, spouses, and dependents
best_for: Florida residents facing unexpected expenses who need cash same-day and lack access to traditional credit
Consumers with poor credit or no credit history who cannot qualify for personal loans or credit cards
Borrowers who prefer in-person service and physical cash disbursement over online lending platforms
diagnosis: Amscot is best for Florida residents facing genuine emergency cash needs who have limited access to traditional credit and value physical locations with extended hours. The primary caveat is that payday loans and installment cash advances are high-cost emergency borrowing products; consumers should exhaust other options first and carefully assess their ability to repay to avoid debt cycles.
